{"notes":"Already fully implemented! The restart_service IPC task type exists end-to-end:

• Rust enum variant: IpcTask::RestartService in intercom-core/src/ipc.rs
• Handler with SIGTERM + rate limiting (60s): intercomd/src/ipc.rs handle_restart_service()
• Authorization: main-group-only check in authorize_task()
• Container-side function: ipc-tools.ts restartService()
• MCP tool: restart_service registered in ipc-mcp-stdio.ts
• Available as mcp__intercom__restart_service in agent sessions

No code changes needed.","status":"closed"}

{"id":"Demarch-q52","title":"Add restart_service IPC task type for self-restart","description":"Add a new IPC task type that allows the main group agent to trigger a graceful restart of intercomd. The daemon picks up a {"type": "restart_service"} JSON file, sends itself SIGTERM, and systemd auto-restarts it.

Requirements:
- New IPC task variant in Rust code
- Main group only (security check)
- Rate limiting to prevent restart loops
- Audit logging
- Graceful shutdown (drain queue before stopping)
- Follow the same pattern as refresh_groups IPC task","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:38:50Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:38:50Z"}

Sprint recording fully implemented: 7 tasks complete, 8 files changed, 413 insertions. Lazy auto-start, dispatch v2, artifact hashing, verdict sync, history CLI, incremental cursor, all tests passing.

{"id":"Demarch-ekh","title":"New plugin: interhelm — agent-as-operator runtime diagnostics","description":"Interverse plugin that teaches agents to observe and control running applications via diagnostic HTTP servers and CLI tools. Four core patterns: Health (structured pass/fail per subsystem), Diff (snapshot state, take action, show deltas), Assert (scriptable verification expressions), Smoke Test (end-to-end flow verification). Reference implementation from Shadow Work (sw-agent + Rust debug server). Standalone, framework-agnostic. GitHub issue #7.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T23:14:38Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-09T23:14:38Z"}

{"id":"iv-6bwm7","title":"Research: measure agent idle time and coordination overhead in recent sprints","description":"Problem validation prerequisite for WCM patterns 3 (commitments) and 4 (idle-time work). Before committing sprint resources, measure: (1) idle-agent time per sprint, (2) interface-wait delays, (3) coordination overhead in last 10 closed sprints. Query interstat DB and sprint artifacts. If idle-agent time is \u003c10% of sprint duration, deprioritize Pattern 4. If interface-wait delays are rare, deprioritize Pattern 1 code work. Results gate P2 → P1 promotion for patterns 3 and 4.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T00:56:16Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-02T00:56:16Z","metadata":"\"\\\"{}\\\"\""}

old_value:
{"id":"Demarch-4wm","title":"Design token-efficient conversation resumption across container sessions","description":"When the daemon restarts (or a container session ends for any reason), the agent loses all live conversation context. The next session starts fresh. We need a mechanism to resume conversations efficiently without replaying entire conversation histories into the context window.

Current state:
- Conversations are logged to conversations/ folder (full markdown logs)
- CLAUDE.md is loaded every session (persistent instructions)
- No structured "session handoff" mechanism exists

Requirements:
- Must be token-efficient (can't just dump full conversation history into context)
- Should preserve enough context to continue mid-task work
- Should handle both planned restarts (e.g., after code changes) and unexpected session drops
- Should integrate with the existing memory/file system

Ideas to explore:
- Pre-restart "handoff note" — a structured summary written before triggering restart (what we were doing, what's left, key decisions made)
- Automatic conversation summarization on session end
- A "session state" file that captures active tasks, recent decisions, and pending work
- Tagging system so the next session knows to look for a handoff note
- Using the todo list / beads as implicit session state (already partially works)
- Compact structured format vs prose summaries for token efficiency","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:42:20Z","created_by":"mistakeknot","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:42:20Z"}

{"id":"iv-kpus","title":"Fix phase token double-counting in sprint_record_phase_tokens","description":"sprint_record_phase_tokens queries interstat with WHERE session_id=... but no time or phase boundary. After phase 2, it records tokens_brainstorm + tokens_strategized as the strategized phase total. Every phase after the first is double-counted, making per-stage budget analysis wrong for any multi-phase sprint. Fix: track a cumulative cursor (last recorded total) and record only the delta per phase.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mistakeknot@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T16:38:59Z","created_by":"mk","updated_at":"2026-02-22T16:38:59Z","metadata":"\"\\\"{}\\\"\"","work_type":"mutex"}

new_value: Fixed: added cumulative cursor (token_cursor state key) to compute per-phase deltas instead of re-querying cumulative session total each time

Add to Intercore or Clavain.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mistakeknot","created_at":"2026-03-05T20:30:10Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T20:30:10Z","metadata":"\"\\\"{}\\\"\""}
